note_html:
Subjective:
The patient is a 30-year-old male who injured his right knee while participating in a trampoline activity at a trampoline park. He reports hearing a snap and experiencing instant pain in the knee. He describes the pain as located on the outside of the knee, lateral aspect. He denies taking any medication for the pain except for Tylenol, which provided minimal relief. He has been elevating the knee and has not attempted to ice or use heat. He has not measured his blood pressure regularly and has not obtained a blood pressure cuff as recommended. He reports taking his prescribed lisinopril most days, but not consistently. He has been checking his blood sugar levels 4-5 times a week, but has not been consistent with this. He reports a decent diet, but could improve. He denies being on a specific diet such as the keto diet.

Past medical history:
The patient has hypertension and diabetes. He is currently taking 20mg of lisinopril and metformin 500mg daily. He reports having a high blood pressure reading of 180/80.

Review of systems:
The patient denies any other symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.

Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.

Objective:
Vital signs: 
- Blood pressure: 180/80
- Pulse rate: Not mentioned
- Oxygen saturation: Not mentioned
- Temperature: Not mentioned

Physical exam:
The patient's heart has a regular rate and rhythm with no murmur. His lungs sound clear bilaterally. The knee examination reveals ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee, but no effusion. He has a slight limp and can bear weight, but has a small gait disturbance. He has a decent range of motion, but experiences pain with some movement.

Test Results:
The patient's right knee x-ray shows no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.

Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee. He also has hypertension that requires further evaluation and possible medication modification, and diabetes that requires further evaluation with an A1c and lipid panel.

Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling related to the lateral ligament strain. I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury. I continued the lisinopril at 20mg daily and ordered an echo to evaluate the patient's blood pressure. I also ordered an A1c and lipid panel to further evaluate the patient's diabetes.

Follow-up:
The patient will follow up in 1-2 weeks to discuss the results of the echo, A1c, and lipid panel, and to reassess the patient's knee.
